originalTitle: France’s attacking evolution under Didier Deschamps passes latest Morocco test | Raphaël Jucobin
summary: With the head coach having released the handbrake his side were far superior than they were against the same opponents in Qatar four years ago
content: The scorelines may have been exactly the same, but the stylistic gulf in France’s two World Cup knockout performances against Morocco could not have been greater. In the space of just a single tournament cycle, Didier Deschamps has embraced a vastly different style, one based on a freeform attacking line. As a one-two punch from Kylian Mbappé and Ousmane Dembélé sent the team into this year’s semi-final, the 57-year-old’s tactical reinvention was once again vindicated. In 2022, the France head coach had set up in characteristically pragmatic fashion, with his team managing to blunt Morocco’s attacks in a tightly-contested semi-final in Qatar. Les Bleus won by converting two of their three shots on target across the match, all the while keeping that year’s breakout team at bay. By half-time in Boston three and a half years later, France had managed four times as many efforts on Yassine Bounou’s goal as they did across the entire match in 2022. It was France’s turn to be frustrated. A high pressing line, one of the major stylistic innovations compared to recent France teams, nevertheless kept Morocco on the back foot for most of the first half. Left without an attacking focal point in Ismael Saibari’s absence, the Atlas Lions had little reprieve from the onslaught of attacks and offered little going forward themselves. There will have been fears that the bruising win over Paraguay five days earlier had stunted the momentum of the French attack, which had been operating at a rate of over three goals a game until then. Having been left without ideas by a combative defence, there was a sense that the provocative South American side, while aided by neglectful refereeing, had laid the blueprint to frustrating Les Bleus. The bitter aftertaste of that match lingered for several days, not least given the racist abuse and repeated verbal attacks the Paraguayan senator Celeste Amarilla has aimed at Mbappé. “Morocco will be our opponent, not the referee,” Deschamps insisted on the eve of the match. And while Les Bleus did enjoy a more even-handed officiating performance and a less provocative opponent on Thursday compared to their battle in Philadelphia, Morocco’s defence posed a similarly stern test. A familiar sense of exasperation appeared to set in as the first half went on, with Bounou denying Désiré Doué and the rest of the forwards struggling to connect with each other. The three-minute delay before Mbappé could take his ill-fated penalty midway through the half would evidently add to the feeling of irritation for France. While the Paraguay defence had conceded precious little space to their opponents, Morocco were less conservative in their approach. Mbappé found an opening behind his club teammate Achraf Hakimi, which led to the penalty, while the captain created space for himself on the edge of the box before breaking the deadlock on the hour mark. As was the case against Sweden, the captain’s bolt from the blue opened the scoring after a relatively wasteful display in front of goal. Dembélé’s strike minutes later appeared to hint at an opening of the floodgates, only for a heel issue to bring a premature end to Mbappé’s evening and in turn break up France’s attack. Les Bleus would end the match having had less time on the ball than their opponents, as was the case in their previous match-up, but the momentum of the match never looked like swinging against them this time round. In midfield, Manu Koné’s all-action performance will have secured him one of the few spots in the starting XI which were still open to debate, and laid to rest any worries concerning that arose from Aurélien Tchouaméni’s fitness issues. The defence has scarcely been tested over the last three hours of play. . It’s a situation which suits Les Bleus given William Saliba’s lingering back issues, but it does mean they enter uncharted territory in the final four. Both the Spanish and Belgian attacks will be sharper and less forgiving than any of their opponents so far. Morocco were invariably pinned back as France suffocated the best team they have faced so far in the tournament. For all of their struggles in front of goal, Les Bleus rarely felt threatened by their opponents. “We felt that they weren’t dangerous in the moments when we left them the ball,” Adrien Rabiot told French broadcaster M6 after the match. “We felt as though we didn’t have to fear them.” Not for the first time at this World Cup, France’s attack took at least a dozen chances before finally breaking through. For all the telepathic interplay between Mbappé, Dembélé, and Michael Olise, there is a sense that the sheer quantity of chances they have created has masked their wayward finishing. In his pre-match press conference, Deschamps conceded that his team could still improve in front of goal. “The better the quality of the opponent, the more clinical you have to be,” he underlined. As Les Bleus prepare to leave the East Coast for the first time since their arrival in the US for their semi-final in Dallas, there is a sense that they will be leaving the comfort zone they have established both on and off the pitch. At the moment, though, they appear ready to face any challenge.
chineseTitle: 德尚领导下的法国进攻演变通过了摩洛哥的最新考验拉斐尔·朱科宾
chineseSummary: 在主教练松开手刹的情况下，他的球队比四年前在卡塔尔对阵同样的对手时要优越得多
chineseContent: 法国队两次世界杯淘汰赛对阵摩洛哥队的比分可能完全相同，但风格上的差距却大得不能再大了。在仅仅一个锦标赛周期的时间内，迪迪埃·德尚就采用了一种截然不同的风格，一种基于自由进攻线的风格。随着基利安·姆巴佩和奥斯曼·登贝莱的一记二拳将球队送入今年的半决赛，这位 57 岁的球员的战术革新再次得到了证明。 2022年，这位法国主教练以典型的务实风格进行了部署，他的球队在卡塔尔一场激烈的半决赛中成功地削弱了摩洛哥的进攻。法国队在整场比赛中三脚射门中两脚命中目标，从而获胜，同时将当年的突破球队拒之门外。三年半后，到波士顿的中场休息时，法国队在亚辛·布努的进球上的射门次数是 2022 年整场比赛的四倍。现在轮到法国队感到沮丧了。与最近的法国队相比，高位逼抢线是主要的风格创新之一，但在上半场的大部分时间里，摩洛哥队都处于不利地位。由于伊斯梅尔·塞巴里缺席，阿特拉斯雄狮队失去了进攻焦点，他们几乎无法从猛烈的进攻中得到缓解，也几乎没有什么前进的动力。有人担心，五天前对巴拉圭的惨痛胜利阻碍了法国队进攻的势头，此前法国队的进攻速度一直是场均超过三个进球。好斗的防守让他们毫无头绪，有一种感觉，挑衅的南美队在裁判疏忽的帮助下，已经为令人沮丧的法国队奠定了蓝图。那场比赛的苦涩余味一直萦绕在许多人的心头
whyItMatters: 这条信息属于全球新闻信号，适合用于观察外部环境、政策变化、地缘风险和市场情绪。
